title: Reliable Sync control frames
description: Reliable-sync control frames (protocol.md § Reliable Sync, #lzsync). Two new externally-tagged IpcMessage variants carry the receiver→sender control channel that makes delivery gap-recoverable and at-least-once: ResyncRequest (the ResyncCoordinator asks for a covering Snapshot on a detected gap) and OutboxAck (the receiver advances the sender's DurableOutbox retention cursor and carries the reconnect resume cursor). Both are ordinary framed messages on the negotiated codec (json reference + msgpack cross-language default). The liveness OR-set/LWW cells ride the existing CrdtSync plane (schemas/distributed.json), not a new frame.

ResyncRequest.title: ResyncRequest
ResyncRequest.description: Emitted by a ResyncCoordinator that detected a gap (an inbound Delta with base_epoch > last_epoch). Requests a fresh Snapshot covering `from_epoch`. At most one is outstanding per detected gap (the coordinator stays in its resyncing sub-state until a covering Snapshot lands).

from_epoch.description: The receiver's current last_epoch; the sender's SnapshotProvider returns Snapshot { epoch >= from_epoch }.

OutboxAck.title: OutboxAck
OutboxAck.description: Emitted by a receiver to prove receipt through `through_epoch`. The sender's DurableOutbox may prune retained frames with epoch <= through_epoch (ack_through). On a reconnect handshake this doubles as the resume cursor: the sender replay_from(through_epoch) re-sends every retained frame with epoch > through_epoch. This is a retention/cursor signal, NOT a domain delivery-success authority (that is a CausalReceipt).

through_epoch.description: Highest epoch the receiver has fully applied (its ResyncCoordinator.last_epoch).
